articles.title: Israel says it hit 135 Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon in last 24 hours
articles.summary: Israel says it hit 135 Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon in last 24 hours The Israeli army has claimed to have attacked 135 “Hezbollah targets” over the last day in its renewed strikes on the region, in violation of the ceasefire. The most recent strike has hit an apartment of a residential building in southern Beirut, which the Israeli military has described as a “precision strike” thought to be a targeted assassination.  This is the second strike in Beirut since mid-April, the first of which occurred on 6 May, when Israel said it was targeting a Hezbollah commander. articles.summary: Hamas warns Gaza ceasefire risks collapse after string of Israeli assassinations Hamas on Thursday warned that the ceasefire agreement in Gaza is at risk of collapse following an escalation in Israeli air strikes this month. The Palestinian group said at least 20 people were killed over the past two days in a fresh wave of bombardment targeting residential homes. The statement came after an Israeli air strike hit a building in Gaza City on Wednesday night during the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Adha. articles.title: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack on its territory
articles.summary: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack on its territory Kuwait’s foreign ministry has condemned an Iranian drone-and-missile attack as being a “dangerous escalation” of tensions in the region. The ministry demanded that Iran immediately and unconditionally halt its attacks, which it called a violation of its sovereignty and security. Iran has said that this attack was in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on areas near the Hormuz port city of Bandar Abbas during ongoing peace negotiations.

articles.title: Israeli spy Jonathan Pollard suggests Egypt and Turkey are next targets for war
articles.summary: Israeli spy Jonathan Pollard suggests Egypt and Turkey are next targets for war Israeli-American spy Jonathan Pollard has suggested Israel may attack Egypt and Turkey in the near future. Speaking on a podcast for news outlet Arutz Sheva, Pollard suggested Israel would need to prepare for further wars in the Middle East, after Iran. "I'm not so sure that we will have as easy a time with the Turks as we've had with the Iranians," he said. "We have to be prepared for the next war, which will probably be against Turkey and Egypt. articles.title: Over 250 artists urge UK theatres to end Bloomberg Philanthropies over ties to Israel
articles.summary: Over 250 artists urge UK theatres to end Bloomberg Philanthropies over ties to Israel More than 250 cultural figures, including Caryl Churchill, Alex Lawther and Miriam Margolyes are calling on five leading British theatres to end their partnerships with Bloomberg Philanthropies over its ties to Israel. In an open letter published on Thursday by group Artists for Palestine UK, the figures accuse the National Theatre, the Royal Shakespeare Company, the Royal Court, the Young Vic and the Almeida of “actively undermining” their own ethical commitments in the partnership. articles.title: Israeli prosecutors indict senior Netanyahu aide
articles.summary: Israeli prosecutors indict senior Netanyahu aide The Israeli attorney general’s office announced on Thursday the indictment of a senior adviser to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on charges of “transmitting classified information with the intent to harm state security”. The case involves the leak of a classified document to the German newspaper Bild in 2024, which aimed to support Netanyahu's claim that Hamas was not interested in a ceasefire deal, in order to justify Israel's continued military attacks on Gaza. The alleged leak was in violation of military censorship.

S&P 500 futures slipped 0.2%, pointing to the benchmark’s first decline in more than a week. Brent rose 2.9% toward $97 a barrel after the US carried out airstrikes on an Iranian military site, clouding hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough. The latest upheaval between the US and Iran shows the fragility of the ceasefire and the push for a deal that traders have long seen as only a matter of time. articles.title: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon at least 11, including two children
articles.summary: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon at least 11, including two children Lebanon’s health ministry said on Thursday that Israeli strikes on the south of the country have killed at least 11 people, including two children, and wounded 21 others. One air strike on a building in the city of Sidon killed five people, including two women, and wounded 21, of whom five were children, according to a statement from the ministry.
